items.summary: Needing a break, we honor the rare sweet sliver of comity during Monday's Boston Marathon when two runners, both on course to achieve their personal best, instead stopped to help Ajay Haridasse, collapsed on the ground and unable to stand back up, over the finish line just ahead - because, they explained, "This is what it's all about...Two is better than one." Hallelujah: For now, still human afte

items.title: Palestinian medical student in Cuba speaks to FRFI
items.summary: Whilst in Havana for the Nuestra América international convoy, FRFI met with students from the Latin American School of Medicine (ELAM). The following is an abridged version of our conversation with Ihab, a Palestinian student at ELAM who spoke to us about Cuban internationalism, healthcare under Cuban socialism, the devastating effects of the blockade and […] The post Palestinian medical student

items.title: Shield of the Americas: a summit of sell-outs
items.summary: Following the cancellation of the ‘Summit of the Americas’ scheduled in late 2025 due to growing divides over US intervention in Venezuela, on 7 March Trump founded a new Latin America summit – the ‘Shield of the Americas’. Purportedly to fight ‘narco-terrorism’, the Shield of the Americas is nothing but a poorly masked initiative to […] The post Shield of the Americas: a summit of sell-outs appea

items.title: In Dharavi, Kumbharwada’s clay economy meets the redevelopment chokehold
items.summary: The Dharavi Redevelopment Project threatens to destroy Kumbharwada’s centuries-old pottery. It’s a survey that doesn’t see upper floors or shared kilns.

items.title: Dwarka forest and the misunderstood politics of deemed forests and invasive species
items.summary: The Supreme Court’s dismissal of a challenge to the clearance of over thousands of trees near Delhi’s airport raises hard questions about what counts as a forest when the law privileges planning documents over ecological reality.

items.title: What Cuba’s filmmakers built that Trump cannot sanction away
items.summary: For six decades, Cuban directors made films that outlasted every embargo. Tomás Gutiérrez Alea showed why art and politics cannot be separated.

items.title: Georgia Deports Azerbaijani Journalist, Bypassing European Court Ruling
items.summary: The deportation of a dissident editor, which rights groups condemned as a hallmark of authoritarian cooperation, occurred just a day before Azerbaijan’s president arrived in Tbilisi for a state visit.

items.title: Politically Connected Crypto Project Pursued Resort With Alleged Scam Syndicate Figures
items.summary: AB is a little-known blockchain network that recently partnered with the Trump family’s crypto firm. But one of the projects it has promoted — a proposed crypto technology resort in Southeast Asia — involved people sanctioned by the U.S. last year for ties to the Prince Group, an alleged multibillion-dollar fraud syndicate.

items.title: Greek Premier Reshuffles Government Amid EU Subsidy Scandal
items.summary: Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis is asking European prosecutors to name the lawmakers accused of siphoning millions in agricultural funds for phantom livestock and ineligible farmland.

items.title: Israel blocks US student protesters from entering West Bank
items.summary: Officials at Allenby crossing hint they have access to American police records.

items.title: Incubator babies saved from Gaza genocide reunited with their moms
items.summary: Israeli forces shoot and kill 15-year-old child in Dheisheh refugee camp, occupied West Bank.

items.title: Despite European Extradition Requests, Guinea Keeps Convicted Robber in Prison
items.summary: Both Belgium and the Netherlands have asked Guinea to extradite Ibrahim Akhlal, but he remains in prison in the West African country — even though his sentence has expired.

items.title: Suffocated on the outskirts of Jerusalem
items.summary: Kufr Aqab residents face limited mobility and occupation raids.

items.title: Nepal Arrests Alleged Power Broker Deepak Bhatta in Money Laundering Probe
items.summary: Police in Nepal detained the prominent executive over multimillion-dollar financial irregularities just days after immigration officials barred him from leaving the country.
